edit.groupHint: Wrap selection in a group frame (Ctrl+G)
edit.ungroupHint: Remove group frame and keep children (Ctrl+Shift+G)

canvas.pinBadge: Pinned output (gcPin): runner may skip process and use saved processResult.
canvas.stepCacheBadge: Step cache enabled (data.stepCache): this task may reuse disk cache across runs when Run bar step cache is on.
canvas.errorOutHandle: On error — run follows this path after task, graph_ref, or mcp_tool failure (task retries exhausted).
canvas.placeholder: Canvas — React Flow and graph editing come in phase 4 (see doc/DEVELOPMENT_PLAN.md).
canvas.addNodeNestedEmptyWorkspace: No graphs in the linked workspace folder — link graphs/ or pick another category.
canvas.groupSelectionNotPossible: Cannot group this selection. Select at least two nodes that are not start, comment frames, or group frames.
canvas.ungroupNotPossible: Could not ungroup. Select a single group frame.
canvas.onlyOneStart: This graph already has a start node.

flowControls.interactivity: Lock or unlock dragging, connecting, and selection

canvas.findNodeFilterPh: Filter by id, title, type, graph id…
canvas.findNodeShortcut: Ctrl+F or Ctrl+K — arrow keys — Enter to focus
canvas.findNodeTruncated: Showing first {{count}} matches — narrow your filter

structure.noStart: No start node (runner expects a single start).
structure.startHasIncoming: Start node {{id}} has incoming edges (should have none).
structure.unreachableNodes: Nodes not reachable from start (static; all edges treated as possible): {{ids}}.
structure.mergeFewInputs: Merge node {{id}} has {{count}} incoming branch edge(s) from non-frame nodes (usually 2+ after a split).
structure.forkFewOutputs: Fork node {{id}} has {{count}} unconditional outgoing branch(es) (use 2+ for fan-out).
structure.barrierMergeOutErrorIncoming: Barrier merge {{mergeId}} has an incoming out_error edge {{edgeId}} (not supported; use passthrough merge for error paths).
structure.barrierMergeNoSuccessIncoming: Barrier merge {{id}} has no success-path (out_default) incoming edges from non-frame nodes.
structure.graphRefWorkspaceCycle: Project graphs contain a graph_ref cycle (nested workflows would recurse): {{cycle}}. Remove or retarget graph_ref links.
structure.aiRouteNoOutgoing: AI route node {{id}} has no outgoing branches to executable nodes (add out_default edges).
structure.aiRouteMissingDescriptions: AI route node {{id}}: {{missing}} of {{total}} outgoing branches lack data.routeDescription (routing quality may suffer).
structure.mcpToolEmptyToolName: MCP tool node {{id}} has an empty toolName.
structure.mcpToolStdioMissingCommand: MCP tool node {{id}} (stdio): set command or argv to spawn the MCP server.
structure.mcpToolHttpEmptyUrl: MCP tool node {{id}} (streamable HTTP): serverUrl is empty.
structure.mcpToolUnknownTransport: MCP tool node {{id}} has unknown transport {{transport}}.
structure.llmAgentEmptyCommand: LLM agent node {{id}} has no command or argv (subprocess would not start).
structure.schemaVersionMismatch: Root schemaVersion is {{root}} but meta.schemaVersion is {{meta}}. Use Save to align them (export uses a single version).

inspector.heading: Inspector
inspector.hint: Click a node or edge; Shift+click or drag a selection rectangle for multiple nodes. With nothing selected: edit graph title, id, I/O contract. Drag between handles to connect. Delete/Backspace removes the selection. Ctrl+C / Ctrl+V copy and paste selected nodes (JSON clipboard). Right-click canvas adds a node.
inspector.multiHint: Multiple nodes selected. Edit JSON per node is not available in this mode.
inspector.clipboardCopyFailed: Could not write to the clipboard (browser permissions).
inspector.clipboardInvalid: Clipboard does not contain GraphCaster graph nodes (expected our JSON format).
inspector.commentFrameHint: Sticky note frame (editor-only). Drag nodes into the frame (or create them inside) so they move with the frame. Resize with the handles. Use JSON data "title", "width", "height".
inspector.groupFrameHint: Group frame (editor-only, not a workflow step). Same nesting as sticky: drag nodes inside or use Edit → Group selection. Resize with handles. JSON: "title", "width", "height".
inspector.label: Title
inspector.invalidDataJson: Data must be a JSON object.
inspector.edgeConditionHint: Runtime picks the first edge with no condition or a true condition (see Python runner). In templates and JSON Logic, $json is the predecessor (last_result). $node addresses node outputs like n8n: bracketed node id or shortId, then optional dot-separated fields — details in python/README.md.
inspector.mergeModeHint: Barrier waits until every predecessor has finished (n8n-style join). Use a Fork node to fan out. Paths are still run one-after-another in one process.
inspector.edgeRouteDescriptionHint: Shown to the routing model as this outgoing option (max 1024 chars). If empty, the runner sends a short default label using the target node id.
inspector.mcpCommandHint: Optional when argv is set in JSON. Same splitting rules as task command.
inspector.mcpServerUrlHint: Use https; http only for localhost with “allow insecure localhost”.
inspector.mcpBearerEnvKeyHint: Optional. If empty and envKeys has one entry, that variable supplies Bearer for HTTP.
inspector.llmAgentCommandHint: Same rules as task: split like shlex. Or set argv in JSON data.
inspector.llmAgentMaxStepsHint: 0 = unlimited. Runner kills the child after this many agent_step lines.
inspector.llmAgentEnvKeysHint: Workspace secrets from .graphcaster/workspace.secrets.env (F8), same as task.
inspector.llmAgentInputPayloadInvalid: inputPayload must be a JSON object.
inspector.mcpArgumentsInvalid: Arguments must be a JSON object.
inspector.runUntilThisNodeHint: From the graph start; stops after this node when reached. Unreachable target or earlier failure → failed; exit node → success; nested graph_ref runs to completion. Partial only when stop triggers on the root path (desktop).
inspector.openGraphRefNeedWorkspace: Link a project folder so nested graphs can be opened from graphs/.
inspector.graphRefPreviewHint: Loads file contents only when you expand details (like n8n/Dify lazy child resolution). Index lines below come from workspace scan; details require a read.
inspector.graphRefPreviewDuplicateId: Workspace index lists more than one file with this graph id; preview uses the first match.
inspector.graphRefPreviewErrorParseDoc: File is not a valid GraphCaster graph document.
inspector.graphRefPreviewErrorRead: Could not read the graph file.
inspector.graphRefPreviewErrorUnknown: Target graph id is not in the workspace index (link project folder / save child to graphs/).
inspector.graphInputsHint: Optional contract for callers, e.g. [{"name":"x","type":"string"}]. Leave empty to omit.
inspector.graphOutputsHint: Optional declared outputs. Leave empty to omit.
inspector.graphParamsInvalidJson: Inputs and outputs must be JSON arrays or objects.
inspector.graphSchemaInvalid: Schema version must be an integer.
inspector.pinEnabled: Use pinned output (skip process when payload has processResult)
inspector.pinHint: Like n8n pinData: gcPin.payload is merged into node outputs; if enabled and processResult is present, the runner short-circuits. Values in --context-json for this node id override the document pin.
inspector.pinPayloadLarge: Pinned payload is large (~{{kb}} KiB serialized). Consider trimming stdout/stderr before committing.
inspector.stepCacheEnabled: Eligible for cross-run disk cache (requires Run bar + workspace root)
inspector.stepCacheHint: Like n8n dirtyNodeNames: the next successful desktop run start sends every affected task id in --step-cache-dirty (transitive downstream along success edges, not out_error). Editing node data does the same. Node ids must not contain commas (CSV). Toggle “Step cache” in the Run bar and set workspace root (runs/). Tasks must use data.stepCache to participate.
inspector.cursorAgentPromptFile: Prompt file (relative to cwd base, UTF-8)
inspector.cursorAgentCwdWorkspace: Workspace root (parent of graphs/ if folder is named graphs)
inspector.cursorAgentExtraArgs: Extra args (JSON array of strings, before prompt)
inspector.cursorAgentApplyFiles: Allow file changes (--force with print mode)
inspector.cursorAgentHint: command/argv on the same task overrides this preset. Placeholders: {{out:NODE_ID.processResult.stdout}} or .stderr. Install agent CLI or set GC_CURSOR_AGENT; do not put API keys in the graph.
inspector.cursorAgentNeedPrompt: Set a non-empty prompt or prompt file.
inspector.cursorAgentExtraArgsInvalid: Extra args must be a JSON array of strings, e.g. ["--flag"].

editor.removedDanglingEdges: Removed {{count}} edge(s) with missing endpoints (not in document): {{ids}}.
editor.autosaveFailedBanner: Autosave to workspace failed (disk full, permissions, or transient I/O). Fix the issue and use File → Save, or retry after fixing access.
editor.autosaveFailedConsole: [editor] Autosave failed — workspace write error (see banner). Not more than once per 30s.

warnings.multipleUnconditional: Node {{sourceId}}: more than one outgoing edge with no condition (runner uses JSON order).
warnings.multipleUnconditionalErrorOut: Node {{sourceId}} (on-error out): more than one unconditional edge from the error handle.
warnings.outErrorUnreachable: Node {{sourceId}}: out_error is never used (runner only fails over from task with command/argv or graph_ref).
warnings.invalidSourceHandle: Edge {{edgeId}}: invalid source handle "{{handle}}" on node {{nodeId}} ({{nodeType}}). Python run will reject the graph.
warnings.invalidTargetHandle: Edge {{edgeId}}: invalid target handle "{{handle}}" on node {{nodeId}} ({{nodeType}}). Python run will reject the graph.
warnings.templateConditionUnclosed: Edge {{edgeId}} (from node {{sourceId}}): template is incomplete (unclosed delimiter or invalid dotted path).
warnings.templateConditionTooMany: Edge {{edgeId}} (from node {{sourceId}}): more than 32 path placeholders in one condition.
warnings.templateConditionTooLong: Edge {{edgeId}} (from node {{sourceId}}): condition exceeds maximum length (65536 characters after trim).
warnings.templateConditionInvalid: Edge {{edgeId}} (from node {{sourceId}}): expected a single path in mustaches, or path plus comparison (==, !=, <, <=, >, >=) and a literal.

console.heading: Console
console.stub: Run log / NDJSON stream from the Python runner will appear here.
console.empty: No run output yet. Start a run from the desktop app (native window).
console.noMatchingLines: No lines match the current filter or search.
console.exportVisibleHint: Download visible lines (current filter and search).
console.exportFullHint: Download the full console buffer (ignores filter and search).

runHistory.title: Run history
runHistory.needArtifactsAndGraph: Set workspace root (runs/) and ensure the graph has an id — persisted runs are stored under runs/<graphId>/.
runHistory.empty: No run folders found for this graph yet. Run with workspace root set to persist NDJSON.
runHistory.logTruncated: events.ndjson was read from the tail only (size cap); the first line may be a broken JSON fragment — narrow filters or raise the limit in code if needed.
runHistory.replayGraphIdMismatch: Replay log rootGraphId is "{{logRoot}}" but the open graph id is "{{openRoot}}" — node highlights may not match the canvas.

run.heading: Run
run.desktopOnlyHint: Run is available in the native GraphCaster window (Tauri), not in dev:web only.
run.brokerMissing: Run broker is not reachable at {{path}}. In a second terminal run: cd python && python -m graph_caster serve — then keep npm run dev:web. Optional: VITE_GC_RUN_BROKER_TARGET if the broker is not on 127.0.0.1:9847.
run.pythonMissing: Python module graph_caster is not available (interpreter: {{path}}). Set GC_PYTHON and install the package, or set GC_GRAPH_CASTER_PACKAGE_ROOT for PYTHONPATH.
run.fixStructureFirst: Fix critical graph structure (valid start, no incoming edges to start) before running. Unreachable-node warnings alone do not block run.
run.cannotSaveDuringRun: Save is disabled while a run is active.
run.stepCacheHint: Passes --step-cache to Python with the workspace root. Tasks opt in via data.stepCache. Badge shows queued dirty count.
run.stepCacheNeedArtifactsHint: Set workspace root (runs/) first — step cache requires --artifacts-base.
run.queuedHost: [host] Run {{runId}} queued — {{position}} in the start queue.
run.pendingQueueHint: {{count}} run(s) waiting for a free slot (FIFO).
run.focusRunLabel: Focused run (console and live canvas updates)
run.brokerFifoQueued: [host] Run broker: queued (FIFO position {{position}}). Streaming starts now; the worker begins when a slot is free.
run.brokerPendingQueueFullHint: Raise GC_RUN_BROKER_PENDING_MAX or reduce burst load; the broker pending FIFO is full.
run.hostConcurrencyHint: Raise GC_RUN_BROKER_MAX_RUNS (web broker) or GC_TAURI_MAX_RUNS (desktop) to match or exceed gc.run.maxConcurrent in localStorage.

console.outputTruncated: Console: {{count}} process_output event(s) dropped (SSE subscriber queue full; one NDJSON line ≈ one event). Persisted run log on disk is unchanged.

openModal.title: Could not open graph
openModal.read_failed: The file could not be read.
openModal.not_object: The file must be a JSON object (not an array or plain value).
openModal.invalid_meta: Property "meta" must be a JSON object when present.
openModal.invalid_viewport: Property "viewport" must be a JSON object when present.
openModal.invalid_schema_version_root: Root "schemaVersion" must be a finite integer (or omit it).
openModal.invalid_schema_version_meta: "meta.schemaVersion" must be a finite integer (or omit it).
openModal.nodes_not_array: Property "nodes" must be a JSON array.
openModal.edges_not_array: Property "edges" must be a JSON array.
openModal.invalid_node_not_object: nodes[{{index}}] must be a JSON object.
openModal.invalid_node_id: nodes[{{index}}].id must be a non-empty string.
openModal.invalid_node_data: nodes[{{index}}].data must be a JSON object when present.
openModal.invalid_node_position: nodes[{{index}}].position must be a JSON object when present.
openModal.invalid_edge_not_object: edges[{{index}}] must be a JSON object.
openModal.invalid_edge_id: edges[{{index}}].id must be a non-empty string.
openModal.invalid_edge_data: edges[{{index}}].data must be a JSON object when present.
openModal.invalid_graph_id_meta: "meta.graphId" must be a string or number when present.
openModal.invalid_graph_id_root: Root "graphId" must be a string or number when present.
openModal.schema_normalize_root: Root "schemaVersion" could not be normalized to an integer.
openModal.schema_normalize_meta: "meta.schemaVersion" could not be normalized to an integer.

saveModal.title: Save graph
saveModal.pickCardHint: Pick a file tile to overwrite it, or type a new file name below.
saveModal.noFilesYet: No JSON files in graphs/ yet — enter a file name to create one.
saveModal.noWorkspaceHint: Project folder is not linked. You will choose where to save (system dialog in Chromium, or browser download). Link a folder to save inside graphs/ with tiles.
saveModal.workspaceUnavailable: The project folder link was lost. Link graphs/ again, or save without a linked folder.
saveModal.documentUnavailable: The graph could not be read from the editor. Try again or reload the page.

workspace.linkedHint: Project folder is linked; graphs/ is used for Save and autosave.
workspace.notSupported: Folder linking needs a Chromium-based browser (File System Access API). Use Save to download otherwise.
workspace.linkFailed: Could not open the project folder or create graphs/.
workspace.writeFailed: Could not write the graph file.
workspace.duplicateGraphId: Another file already uses this graphId: {{file}}
workspace.needLinkForGraphRef: Link the project folder first to open a nested graph from graphs/.
workspace.graphNotInIndex: No graph file with graphId {{id}} was found in graphs/ (rescan after adding files).
